setting.note: Wolfe's panorama of 1980s New York runs on the distance between its worlds. Sherman McCoy, a Wall Street bond trader who thinks of himself as a Master of the Universe, lives in a Park Avenue duplex and takes a wrong turn off the expressway into the South Bronx, where his mistress hits a young Black man with his car. The fallout pulls in the Bronx County Courthouse, a Harlem preacher, and the tabloids, and the map is the city's fault lines laid end to end.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Tom Wolfe, first published 1987. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Bonfire of the Vanities, Tom Wolfe (1987). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The burnt orange of the bonfire in the title, the vanities of money and status going up in flames. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
